response:
**NVIDIA (NVDA) — 8-Quarter Earnings Surprise Streak**

The rendered chart shows NVDA’s actual EPS and estimated EPS across the last 8 quarters, with the surprise delta (actual minus estimated) plotted as bars on a secondary axis. **Every bar is green:** NVDA has beaten consensus estimates for 8 consecutive quarters with no misses.

| Quarter | Fiscal End | Reported EPS | Estimated EPS | Surprise | % Surprise |
|:---|:---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Q2 FY2025 | 2024-07-31 | $0.68 | $0.63 | +$0.05 | +7.94% |
| Q3 FY2025 | 2024-10-31 | $0.81 | $0.75 | +$0.06 | +8.00% |
| Q4 FY2025 | 2025-01-31 | $0.89 | $0.85 | +$0.04 | +4.71% |
| Q1 FY2026 | 2025-04-30 | $0.81 | $0.75 | +$0.06 | +8.00% |
| Q2 FY2026 | 2025-07-31 | $1.05 | $1.01 | +$0.04 | +3.96% |
| Q3 FY2026 | 2025-10-31 | $1.30 | $1.26 | +$0.04 | +3.17% |
| Q4 FY2026 | 2026-01-31 | $1.62 | $1.54 | +$0.08 | +5.19% |
| Q1 FY2027 | 2026-04-30 | $1.87 | $1.77 | +$0.10 | +5.65% |

**At a glance:** The beat streak is perfect across all 8 quarters. The EPS base nearly tripled from $0.68 to $1.87, and the absolute surprise widened in the most recent two reports to +$0.08 and +$0.10.

args.code:
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Raw data from get_full_fundamentals_1
earnings = get_full_fundamentals_1["earnings"]["earnings"]

# Reverse to chronological order (oldest first)
earnings = earnings[::-1]

# Extract fields
fiscal_dates = [e["fiscalDateEnding"] for e in earnings]
reported = [e["reportedEPS"] for e in earnings]
estimated = [e["estimatedEPS"] for e in earnings]
surprise = [e["surprise"] for e in earnings]

# Create readable quarter labels: Q3 FY2025 style (approximate)
# NVDA fiscal year ends in January. So:
# 2024-07-31 -> Q2 FY2025
# 2024-10-31 -> Q3 FY2025
# 2025-01-31 -> Q4 FY2025
# 2025-04-30 -> Q1 FY2026
# 2025-07-31 -> Q2 FY2026
# 2025-10-31 -> Q3 FY2026
# 2026-01-31 -> Q4 FY2026
# 2026-04-30 -> Q1 FY2027
labels = []
for d in fiscal_dates:
    y, m, _ = map(int, d.split("-"))
    if m == 1:
        labels.append(f"Q4 FY{y}")   # Jan
    elif m == 4:
        labels.append(f"Q1 FY{y+1}") # Apr -> next fiscal year
    elif m == 7:
        labels.append(f"Q2 FY{y+1}") # Jul
    elif m == 10:
        labels.append(f"Q3 FY{y+1}") # Oct
    else:
        labels.append(d[:7])

# Colors: green for beat, red for miss
bar_colors = ["#2ecc71" if s > 0 else "#e74c3c" for s in surprise]

# Build the figure
fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(10, 6), sharex=True, gridspec_kw={"height_ratios": [2, 1]})

# Top panel: EPS lines
x = np.arange(len(labels))
ax1.plot(x, reported, marker="o", linewidth=2, color="#2980b9", label="Actual EPS")
ax1.plot(x, estimated, marker="s", linewidth=2, color="#8e44ad", linestyle="--", label="Estimated EPS")
ax1.set_ylabel("EPS ($)")
ax1.set_title("NVIDIA (NVDA) — Last 8 Quarters Earnings Surprise")
ax1.legend(loc="upper left")
ax1.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
ax1.set_ylim(bottom=0)

# Bottom panel: surprise bars
ax2.bar(x, surprise, color=bar_colors, width=0.6, edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5)
ax2.axhline(0, color="black", linewidth=0.8)
ax2.set_ylabel("Surprise ($)")
ax2.set_xticks(x)
ax2.set_xticklabels(labels, rotation=45, ha="right")
ax2.set_xlabel("Fiscal Quarter")
ax2.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis="y")

# Add value labels on bars
for i, (s, c) in enumerate(zip(surprise, bar_colors)):
    ax2.text(i, s + 0.005, f"+{s:.2f}" if s > 0 else f"{s:.2f}", ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=9, color="black")

plt.tight_layout()

save_figure(alt="NVDA earnings surprise chart: actual vs estimated EPS with surprise bars", title="NVDA Earnings Surprise — Last 8 Quarters")

set_result({
    "quarters": labels,
    "reported_eps": reported,
    "estimated_eps": estimated,
    "surprise": surprise,
    "all_beats": all(s > 0 for s in surprise),
    "beat_streak": len(surprise)
})
